name: Sicilian Defence
description: Black’s sharpest reply to 1.e4 — Najdorf plus anti-Sicilian answers.
source: curated repertoire (scripts/repertoires/sicilian.pgn) + lichess explorer, ratings 1400/1600/1800, speeds blitz/rapid

children.comment: Decline: 4.e5 Nd5 lands in the Alapin lines below with an extra option to keep the pawn.
The bishop belongs here: it watches c4 and supports ...b5 and ...d5.
The point of ...a6: this break would lose material without it, now it gains space for free.
Same recipe against the English Attack: ...e5, ...Be6, ...Be7 and queenside play with ...b5.
Against the sharpest try, keep it classical: ...Be7, ...Qc7, ...Nbd7.
Blunt the Sozin bishop first, then continue as usual.
The freeing break: with pieces traded the isolated-pawn positions are simply fine for Black.
The calmest: trade a pair of bishops and inherit an easy game.
The e5 pawn is gone; finish development and target the isolated d4 pawn forever.
Strike at e5 before White consolidates the big centre.
Blunt the c4 bishop; ...Nge7 keeps f5 covered and the long diagonal open.
The standard anti-Closed setup: ...d5 or ...f5 breaks come when White castles.

variations.name: Open - Najdorf
variations.intro: The main event: 2...d6 and 5...a6. The little pawn move keeps b5 from every White piece and prepares ...e5 - the central counterstrike that defines the Najdorf.

variations.name: Moscow (3.Bb5+)
variations.intro: 3.Bb5+ dodges Najdorf theory. Trade the bishops, equalise the position, and outplay the middlegame.

variations.name: Alapin and Smith-Morra
variations.intro: 2.c3 takes the open Sicilian away; ...Nf6 takes the initiative away right back, hitting e4 before c3 pays off. Decline the Morra the same way.

variations.name: Grand Prix and Closed
variations.intro: 2.Nc3 keeps the position closed. Fianchetto, control d4, and let the g7 bishop outlive White's f-pawn storm.

variations.name: Smith-Morra, Siberian Variation — 9.g3! declines the trap
Siberian Trap — punish 9.h3?? with …Nd4! and Qh2#
Anti-Sozin — 6…e6! avoids the Magnus Smith trap
